req.title: Account close
req.statement: A customer shall close an account with zero balance; closing with non-zero balance shall require sweep to another account.
preconditions: account.owner == caller

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-014 defines two primary partitions: (1) zero-balance accounts that may close directly, and (2) non-zero-balance accounts that require a sweep target. Key boundary is balance == 0 (exactly zero triggers the happy path; any deviation requires sweep). State transitions tested: active->closed (happy) and active->rejected (no sweep). Decision table covers the four combinations of balance and sweep presence. Negative paths cover missing sweep and wrong caller. All tests use fixture-injected HTTP clients and pre-seeded accounts to remain idempotent; each test seeds its own account and cleans up via the close operation itself or a teardown fixture call.

tests.name: test_close_account_zero_balance_succeeds
preconditions: Caller is authenticated as the account owner
Account exists in state=active with balance=0.00
No pending transactions on account
actions: Assert final_statement URL is present in response
tests.executable:
def test_close_account_zero_balance_succeeds(api_client, zero_balance_account):
    account_id = zero_balance_account["id"]
    resp = api_client.post(f"/accounts/{account_id}/close")
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["state"] == "closed"
    assert body.get("final_statement") is not None
    get_resp = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{account_id}")
    assert get_resp.json()["state"] == "closed"

tests.name: test_close_account_nonzero_balance_without_sweep_returns_409
preconditions: Caller is authenticated as the account owner
Account exists in state=active with balance=150.00
No sweep target provided in request body
actions: Assert error code indicates sweep required
trace: R-FIN-014: error pathway 409 when balance != 0 without sweep
tests.executable:
def test_close_account_nonzero_balance_without_sweep_returns_409(api_client, nonzero_balance_account):
    account_id = nonzero_balance_account["id"]
    resp = api_client.post(f"/accounts/{account_id}/close", json={})
    assert resp.status_code == 409
    body = resp.json()
    assert "sweep" in body.get("error_code", "").lower() or "sweep" in body.get("message", "").lower()
    get_resp = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{account_id}")
    assert get_resp.json()["state"] == "active"

tests.name: test_close_account_balance_exactly_zero_boundary
preconditions: Caller is authenticated as the account owner
Account balance is exactly 0.00 (not 0.01, not -0.01)
Account state=active
expected: HTTP 200 OK at the exact boundary value of balance=0.00
trace: R-FIN-014: zero balance is the exact boundary enabling direct close
BVA: balance=0.00 is lower boundary of the zero-balance equivalence class
tests.executable:
def test_close_account_balance_exactly_zero_boundary(api_client, create_account_with_balance):
    account = create_account_with_balance(balance="0.00")
    account_id = account["id"]
    resp = api_client.post(f"/accounts/{account_id}/close")
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["state"] == "closed"
    assert body.get("final_statement") is not None

tests.name: test_close_account_nonzero_balance_with_valid_sweep_succeeds
preconditions: Caller is authenticated as the account owner
Source account exists in state=active with balance=200.00
Sweep target account exists, is active, and owned by same customer
No pending transactions on source account
actions: Assert target account balance increased by 200.00
expected: target account.balance increased by source balance
trace: R-FIN-014: non-zero balance close shall require sweep to another account
DT: balance!=0 AND sweep_provided => close succeeds
tests.executable:
def test_close_account_nonzero_balance_with_valid_sweep_succeeds(api_client, nonzero_balance_account, sweep_target_account):
    source_id = nonzero_balance_account["id"]
    target_id = sweep_target_account["id"]
    source_balance = float(nonzero_balance_account["balance"])
    target_balance_before = float(api_client.get(f"/accounts/{target_id}").json()["balance"])
    resp = api_client.post(f"/accounts/{source_id}/close", json={"sweep_account_id": target_id})
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["state"] == "closed"
    assert body.get("final_statement") is not None
    target_after = float(api_client.get(f"/accounts/{target_id}").json()["balance"])
    assert abs(target_after - (target_balance_before + source_balance)) < 0.01

tests.name: test_close_account_as_non_owner_returns_403
preconditions: Account exists in state=active owned by customer A
Caller is authenticated as customer B (not the owner)
Account has zero balance
trace: R-FIN-014: precondition account.owner == caller must hold
tests.executable:
def test_close_account_as_non_owner_returns_403(api_client_customer_a, api_client_customer_b, zero_balance_account_owned_by_a):
    account_id = zero_balance_account_owned_by_a["id"]
    resp = api_client_customer_b.post(f"/accounts/{account_id}/close")
    assert resp.status_code == 403
    get_resp = api_client_customer_a.get(f"/accounts/{account_id}")
    assert get_resp.json()["state"] == "active"

tests.name: test_close_account_state_transition_active_to_closed
preconditions: Account is in state=active (not already closed or suspended)
Account balance is 0.00
Caller is authenticated as account owner
actions: Attempt second POST /accounts/{id}/close and assert idempotency or rejection
expected: Second close attempt returns 409 or 200 idempotently (state remains closed)
trace: STC: covers active->closed transition and closed->closed guard
tests.executable:
def test_close_account_state_transition_active_to_closed(api_client, zero_balance_account):
    account_id = zero_balance_account["id"]
    initial = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{account_id}").json()
    assert initial["state"] == "active"
    resp = api_client.post(f"/accounts/{account_id}/close")
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    assert resp.json()["state"] == "closed"
    after = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{account_id}").json()
    assert after["state"] == "closed"
    second_resp = api_client.post(f"/accounts/{account_id}/close")
    assert second_resp.status_code in (200, 409)
    assert api_client.get(f"/accounts/{account_id}").json()["state"] == "closed"
